other_observers: Betty Minges. She was some distance away from me and farther than me from the bird, so she said she didn't get a good look.; I am the original observer. I don't know that anyone else saw this bird later after I reported it to Colorado Rare Bird Alert.
specific_location: About 2 miles north of Golden city limits, and about a mile west of state highway 93.
description: The bird appeared to be about 4 1/2 inches or so, with a small compact appearance. It was bright yellow on the face with a black eye-line through the eye and a black "comma" or curved line beginning at the eye line and curving down below the eye. The bill was dark and narrow in typical warbler fashion. Its throat, breast and belly were also bright yellow with very pronounced black streaking (broken lines) along the edges of the breast and flanks. Wing and upper parts were dull olive/yellow. I don't recall distinctive wingbars, but what bars were there appeared to be pale double bars in buff or very light yellow. I have seen the similar Pine Warbler several times, and this bird was noticeably smaller with more brightly-colored plumage, and the black markings much more highly contrasting.Behaviors: The bird was in a large mature cottonwood tree between a large open meadow and a creek with riparian understory and mature cottonwood and box elder. He was very animated and quick in his movements, hopping or flying from branch to branch and foraging for insects among the newly unfurled spring leaves. (Spring is late here in Colorado). He worked this one tree for at least a couple of minutes, so I got several brief, but good looks at him when he wasn't disappearing among the leaves. Although I had my camera with a 400mm lense, I could never quite get a good focused shot off due to his quickness and the abundant foliage. He finally flew away to another thicket much farther away. I was never able to find it again. This prairie warbler was seen roughly at the same time my birding partner and I also spotted the unusual Black-throated Blue Warbler (which I did manage to get pictures of).Call: The bird was not vocalizing at the time.Plumage: Bright yellow with black markings
